metadata.title: DanriWeb | Frontend Developer
metadata.description: Welcome to the official DanriWeb website. Here you will find information about my projects, skills in creating modern interfaces, and experience in Frontend development.

metadata.title: Cases
metadata.description: All DanriWeb projects are real tasks, architectural solutions, and measurable results. Frontend development using React and Next.js.

cases.title: Engineering approach in action
cases.description: Real tasks - no tutorials. Every case involves a specific problem, accepted solutions, and measurable results.

metadata.title: Discuss a project

hero.title: Pay for the <result>result</result>, not promises
hero.description: The developer who actually cares. Business-focused with code quality in mind: maintainable solutions and predictable architecture.

about.title: The developer you need

hero.bio1: Hi! I am a Frontend Developer from Kazakhstan with <years>3 years</years> of commercial experience. I entered IT without university or bootcamps. I was inspired by the philosophy and core of development. To this day, I never stop, striving to become the best version of myself every single day.
hero.bio2: When I was trusted to mentor juniors, I realized something important: the value of a developer is not in the amount of knowledge, but in the mindset. The mindset that helps not only you to grow, but the team and the product around you too.
hero.bio3: For me, self-discipline is not a perk, it's a standard. A clear commit, clean architecture, adhering to best practices every day - this is respect for those who come after. Outside of code, I read Korean manga: its philosophy rhymes unexpectedly well with what I value in development.

principles.title: Business, not just code
principles.description: I filter every technical decision through a critical question: what does it give the product and the person behind the screen? Beautiful code itself is not the goal - the goal is a working result.

principles.title: Ask, don't guess
principles.description: Asking the right question before the start is fairer and more effective than confidently doing the wrong thing. Good communication saves more time than any code optimization.

principles.title: Mistakes are fuel
principles.description: I accept mistakes not as a disappointment, but as an excuse to dig deeper. A developer who is not afraid to admit being wrong grows faster than one who protects their solution.

principles.title: Follow, not just know
principles.description: Many say that best practices are the basics. But the hardest part is not learning them, but applying them every day. Consistency in the little things is professionalism.

timeline.title: The beginning of the journey
timeline.description: Started without courses or university. During financial difficulties, I took screenshots of docs to study offline during my part-time job. First project: a weather app via public API.

timeline.title: First commercial tasks
timeline.description: Freelancing via acquaintances and job boards, first startups. Faced real business requirements and learned to turn raw ideas into working code.

timeline.title: Production and Mentorship
timeline.description: Marketplaces, dashboards, active products. For the first time I was asked to mentor juniors - that's when I understood that deep understanding is more effective than sheer volume of knowledge.

timeline.title: Positioning and Growth
timeline.description: Focus on architecture and quality. Managing contract projects, developing my portfolio, and purposefully moving towards a strong product team.

about.description:
I ask the right questions before starting, so there's less to redo after.

This is what development looks like in capable hands.

cases.title: Engineering approach in action
cases.description:
Not just a beautiful UI, but a well-thought-out logic under the hood.

Fast, secure, and supported by best coding practices.

processes.title: Process without surprises

items.title: Idea
items.description: I analyze the task, business goals, and the needs of your audience.

items.title: Strategy
items.description: I pick the optimal tech stack and design the architecture.

items.title: Development
items.description: I write clean, scalable code with regular demonstrations of the result.

items.title: Result
items.description: We launch the project! I'm always in touch for support and further development.

processes.description:
Four stages I go through with every client.

Transparent, humanely, and on time.

form.messagePlaceholder: Tell me a bit about the task...

modal.title: Discuss a project
modal.description: Leave your contacts and we will bring your ideas to life.

10.title: WCAG 2.1 Web Accessibility
10.task: The interface wasn't working with keyboards or screen readers. Partially impaired users simply could not use it.
10.solution: Added ARIA attributes, handled focus management, fixed color contrast. Walked through edge scenarios using axe-core and manually.

0.label: Accessibility
0.description: WCAG 2.1 AA level: focus, contrast, ARIA

2.description: Implemented within the UI kit of a contract project

01.title: Architectural Evolution of a Marketplace
01.task: Legacy monolith with circular dependencies. Release cycle took 2 weeks due to the complexity of refactoring.
01.solution: Migration to FSD, business logic isolation. Migrated from Redux to Zustand. State machines for checkout.

0.label: Architecture
0.description: Full transition to FSD decomposing the legacy monolith

02.title: Brand User Dashboard
02.task: Launch a fully functional user dashboard from scratch in 2 working days.
02.solution: shadcn/ui + Zod validation for reactive forms. Notification system implemented hours before launch.

0.label: Speed
0.description: Full implementation of the user dashboard in 20 hours

1.label: Complexity
1.description: Over 15 complex forms and dynamic user data

2.description: System launched for the sale event without critical bugs

03.title: UI Kit from Scratch
03.task: A project without a design system - every component was written differently, zero reusability.
03.solution: Built a core set of components: buttons, inputs, cards, typography. Documented usage and variants.

0.label: Components
0.description: Core set of reusable UI elements

1.label: Documentation
1.description: Description of states and usage rules for every component

2.description: Developed during an actual freelance project

04.title: Landing Page for Small Business
04.task: A small business without a website. Needed a quick launch with no bloat - just the essence.
04.solution: Next.js layout highlighting fast load times, optimization, and mobile responsiveness. No unnecessary libraries or logic. Just what actually works.

1.label: Speed
1.description: Performance as a priority from day one

05.title: Onboarding & Team Agreements
05.task: New developers spent days trying to understand the project. No agreements existed, everyone coded differently.
05.solution: Established project structure, naming conventions, commit formats, and basic code review principles. Pushed it to the repository for everyone to read.

0.label: Team
0.description: The document was written in a real team, not in a vacuum

1.label: Process
1.description: Commits, naming, code reviews - basic but critical things

06.title: Responsive Interface for Every Screen
06.task: A table with detailed data only displayed correctly on desktop. On mobile, there was a horizontal scroll and lost data.
06.solution: Rebuilt the component: cards on mobile, table on desktop. Pure CSS using media queries without JS.

0.label: Responsive
0.description: Different layouts across screens without JS calculations

2.description: Part of a freelance project for a small business

07.title: Page Load Optimization
07.task: A page packed with heavy components loaded slowly. LCP above 4 seconds - bad for both users and SEO.
07.solution: Component lazy loading, code splitting, image optimization via next/image. Removed blocking requests at startup.

0.label: Performance
0.description: Focus on Core Web Vitals: LCP, CLS, FID

08.title: Authentication & Route Protection
08.task: A project without authorization: anyone could load any page. A token-based system and access control were required.
08.solution: JWT + refresh tokens, protected routes using Next.js middleware. Token storage logic isolated from the UI.

1.label: Architecture
1.description: Authorization is isolated from the UI layer

09.title: Multilingual Support (i18n)
09.task: The product expanded into a new market. All strings were hardcoded in components - adding a second language was impossible without refactoring.
09.solution: Extracted strings to JSON files, integrated next-intl. Organized translation structure so they can be updated by non-developers.

1.label: Structure
1.description: Translations organized in separate files by namespace
